mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut periods over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within a single month signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate equity sell-offs.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the 0.5pp monthly threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, driving sustained positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 60-day forward returns in >50% of occurrences across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side bottlenecks that force immediate short-term equity repricing downward.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of industrial production contractions occurring during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate combined with stable or declining consumer price inflation creates a favorable real yield environment that compresses equity discount rates and lifts forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 20-day returns in >55% of cases when tested over a 4-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month declines in total nonfarm payrolls indicate weakening aggregate demand that systematically depresses corporate earnings expectations and equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >60% of periods following consecutive payroll declines over a 5-year walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP growth rate exceeding 3.0 percent signals robust economic expansion that triggers immediate positive market repricing over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of quarters where real GDP growth surpasses the 3.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase confirms supply-side capacity utilization that supports corporate margins and drives positive one-month equity returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >60% of industrial production expansions occurring within post-recession recovery regimes.
